key_insight: SciPhyRL turns dynamic portfolio optimization into a data-driven pathwise HJB problem solved offline with PINNs, enabling cost-aware multi-period allocation from historical trajectories.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical control-engineering template for large-scale decision systems where transaction costs, volatility, and signal decay must be handled jointly under short-horizon constraints.
method: Extend the state with cumulative costs and formulate the control problem in continuous time.
Project the HJB onto observed paths and solve the resulting pathwise Hamilton-Jacobi equation directly from data using a PINN.
Replace continuous trading rate control with discrete target holdings to realize signal-implied positions immediately and model execution via quadratic price impact.
evidence_signal: On a 14-asset ETF universe with an engineered oracle signal, the learned Gibbs policy outperformed static and myopic baselines out of sample with improved Sharpe ratio.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and realism of the offline signal/data and on the quadratic impact model matching actual market microstructure.
paper_title: SciPhy Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization
